title: Repair Windows startup with bootrec
description: A Windows workstation fails to boot after a power loss. Use Windows Recovery Command Prompt commands to repair boot records and verify startup.

terminal.welcome: The system is in Windows Recovery Environment. The OS volume is present but Windows will not boot.
terminal.unknownCommandOutput: This simulation supports startup repair commands used in this recovery path.

commands.output:
Scanning all disks for Windows installations.

Successfully scanned Windows installations.
Total identified Windows installations: 1
[1] C:\Windows
commands.summary: Confirmed a Windows installation exists and can be discovered from recovery.

evidence.title: Windows Installation Found
evidence.body: The recovery environment found one Windows installation at C:\Windows.

commands.output: The operation completed successfully.
commands.summary: Repaired the master boot record.

commands.output: The operation completed successfully.
commands.summary: Repaired the boot sector.

commands.output:
Scanning all disks for Windows installations.

Total identified Windows installations: 1
Add installation to boot list? Yes
The operation completed successfully.
commands.summary: Rebuilt the Boot Configuration Data store.

evidence.title: BCD Rebuilt
evidence.body: The Windows installation was added back to the boot configuration.

commands.output: The system restarted and booted to the Windows sign-in screen successfully.
commands.summary: Restarted and verified Windows startup.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: Windows booted successfully after bootrec repairs.

commands.output: Access denied. Formatting the OS volume would destroy user data.
commands.summary: Attempted a destructive disk command.

penalty.reason: Formatting the OS volume is destructive and not appropriate for startup repair.

commands.output: Windows Resource Protection could not start the repair service from this recovery prompt.
commands.summary: Tried a system file repair before repairing boot configuration.

penalty.reason: The scenario points to boot configuration repair, not an in-OS system file scan first.

grading.summary: A strong startup repair path confirms the Windows installation, repairs boot records, rebuilds BCD, verifies startup, and documents the result.

title: Check and repair file-system errors with chkdsk
description: A Windows user reports disk errors and corrupted files after an improper shutdown. Use command-line tools to check and schedule a repair safely.

terminal.welcome: You are in an elevated Command Prompt on a booted Windows system. The user reports file errors after a crash.
terminal.unknownCommandOutput: This scenario supports disk checking commands for the repair workflow.

commands.output:
The type of the file system is NTFS.
Windows has scanned the file system and found problems.
Please run chkdsk /f to correct these.
commands.summary: Checked the file system and confirmed repair is needed.

evidence.title: CHKDSK Read-Only Scan
evidence.body: The scan found file-system errors and recommended running CHKDSK with repair.

commands.output:
Cannot lock current drive.
Chkdsk cannot run because the volume is in use by another process.
Would you like to schedule this volume to be checked the next time the system restarts? (Y/N) Y
commands.summary: Scheduled CHKDSK repair for the next restart.

commands.output: The system restarted. CHKDSK ran before Windows loaded and corrected file-system errors.
commands.summary: Restarted so the scheduled disk repair could run.

evidence.title: Repair Completed
evidence.body: CHKDSK ran during startup and corrected file-system errors.

commands.output:
The type of the file system is NTFS.
Windows has scanned the file system and found no problems.
No further action is required.
commands.summary: Verified no remaining file-system errors.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: A follow-up CHKDSK scan reports no remaining file-system problems.

commands.output: Access denied. Formatting would erase the operating system volume.
commands.summary: Attempted destructive remediation.

penalty.reason: Formatting the drive is unsafe for a repairable file-system issue.

commands.output: DiskPart cannot clean the active system disk from the running OS.
commands.summary: Attempted to wipe partition data.

penalty.reason: Cleaning the disk would remove partition data and is not appropriate for CHKDSK repair.

grading.summary: A strong disk-error workflow checks the disk, schedules repair correctly when the volume is in use, restarts, verifies the repair, and documents the result.

title: Troubleshoot a mapped network drive with net use
description: A domain user cannot access a mapped department drive. Use net use commands to inspect, remove, recreate, and verify the mapping.

terminal.welcome: The user reports the P: drive is disconnected. Use mapped-drive commands to correct it.
terminal.unknownCommandOutput: This scenario supports the mapped drive commands needed for this task.

commands.output:
New connections will be remembered.

Status       Local     Remote                    Network
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Disconnected P:        \\OLD-FS\Projects          Microsoft Windows Network
commands.summary: Listed mapped drives and found a stale P: mapping.

evidence.title: Stale Mapping
evidence.body: P: is mapped to an old server path and is disconnected.

commands.output: P: was deleted successfully.
commands.summary: Removed the stale mapped drive.

commands.output: The command completed successfully.
commands.summary: Mapped P: to the correct Projects share persistently.

evidence.title: Drive Mapping
evidence.body: P: now points to the correct \\FS01\Projects share.

commands.output:
New connections will be remembered.

Status       Local     Remote                    Network
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
OK           P:        \\FS01\Projects            Microsoft Windows Network
commands.summary: Verified the corrected drive mapping is connected.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: The P: drive mapping is connected to the correct share.

commands.output:
Windows IP Configuration

Successfully flushed the DNS Resolver Cache.
commands.summary: Flushed DNS without evidence of name resolution failure.

penalty.reason: The mapped drive issue is a stale mapping, not DNS cache failure.

commands.output: All mapped drives were removed.
commands.summary: Removed all mapped drives instead of the affected mapping only.

penalty.reason: Deleting all mappings can disrupt unrelated access.

grading.summary: A strong mapped-drive workflow inspects mappings, removes the stale mapping, recreates the correct persistent mapping, verifies it, and documents the result.

title: Fix Linux script permissions
description: A Linux maintenance script will not run for the operations group. Use Linux commands to inspect ownership and apply least-privilege permissions.

terminal.welcome: The user reports /opt/scripts/backup.sh cannot be executed by the operations group.
terminal.unknownCommandOutput: This scenario supports Linux file permission commands for this task.

commands.output: -rw-r----- 1 root root 1842 Jun 30 08:00 /opt/scripts/backup.sh
commands.summary: Inspected ownership and permissions on the script.

evidence.title: Initial Permissions
evidence.body: The script is owned by root:root and lacks execute permission for the operations group.

commands.summary: Changed group ownership to operations while keeping root as owner.
Granted execute permission to owner and group without opening access to everyone.

commands.output: -rwxr-x--- 1 root operations 1842 Jun 30 08:00 /opt/scripts/backup.sh
commands.summary: Verified ownership and permissions after the change.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: The script is executable by root and the operations group, but not by others.

commands.summary: Granted full permissions to everyone.

penalty.reason: chmod 777 violates least privilege and exposes the script to all users.

commands.summary: Deleted the script instead of fixing permissions.

penalty.reason: Deleting the required script is destructive and does not resolve the permission issue.

grading.summary: A strong Linux permissions path inspects ownership, sets the correct group, applies least-privilege execute permission, verifies the result, and documents the change.
